summary: Remove process-based checks from scoreAssumptionHandling — now 3 outcome-only components (30+35+35)
context: Run d18ab582: baseline always scored 60, all coordination conditions always scored 100. The 40pt gap came entirely from process checks (did session 9 read coordination context? did it document via twining tools?). This violates the "score outcomes not process" principle.
rationale: Replaced 4-component scorer (30+20+30+20) with 3 outcome-only components: (1) session 8 surfaced assumption change in code (+30, kept), (2) session 9 updated preferences model (+35, increased from 30), (3) session 9 implemented multi-channel routing end-to-end (+35, new — checks for channel routing logic, multiple channel types, channel interfaces in diffs). No check references coordination tools or files.

alternatives.option: Keep process checks but reduce their weight to 5pts each
alternatives.reason_rejected: Any weight on process checks creates structural bias — baseline can never earn those points regardless of code quality. Zero process credit is the only unbiased option.

alternatives.option: Replace with LLM judge evaluation of assumption handling quality
alternatives.reason_rejected: Adds cost and latency. The automated patterns can detect multi-channel implementation reliably from diffs. LLM judge can be added later as a refinement.
